title: CMDB Change Audit
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_audit"
| table _time, ci_name, field_name, old_value, new_value, changed_by
| sort -_time
description: Tracking all CI attribute changes supports compliance auditing and helps detect unauthorized configuration changes.
value: Tracking all CI attribute changes supports compliance auditing and helps detect unauthorized configuration changes.
implementation: Ingest CMDB audit records. Track all CI attribute changes. Alert on changes to critical CIs outside change windows. Report on change volume by CI class and source (manual vs discovery). Validate accuracy of discovery updates.
visualization: Table (CI changes), Timeline (change events), Bar chart (changes by CI class), Line chart (change volume trend).
knownFalsePositives: Coverage gaps while onboarding new systems, after mergers, during discovery or field changes, or when teams skip owner updates in busy months.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: CMDB audit trail (sys_audit).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest CMDB audit records. Track all CI attribute changes. Alert on changes to critical CIs outside change windows. Report on change volume by CI class and source (manual vs discovery). Validate accuracy of discovery updates.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_audit"
| table _time, ci_name, field_name, old_value, new_value, changed_by
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**CMDB Change Audit** — Tracking all CI attribute changes supports compliance auditing and helps detect unauthorized configuration changes.

Documented **Data sources**: CMDB audit trail (sys_audit). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:cmdb_audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:cmdb_audit". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Pipeline stage (see **CMDB Change Audit**): table _time, ci_name, field_name, old_value, new_value, changed_by
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ServiceNow configuration item or related CMDB view. Spot-check a few CIs, owners, and relationship fields, then confirm discovery dates if we measure freshness.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (CI changes), Timeline (change events), Bar chart (changes by CI class), Line chart (change volume trend).
grandmaExplanation: We review changes to configuration records, so the history of what moved is as serious as a production change.
